title: avenula alpina
intro: Avenula alpina, also known as alpine oat or alpine meadow-grass, is a species of flowering plant in the grass family. It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues and as an expectorant to relieve congestion. The plant contains flavonoids, saponins, and terpenoids, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, contributing to its health benefits, including soothing coughs and alleviating symptoms of bronchitis and asthma. Avenula alpina can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being dried and used in capsules or as a powder for supplementation.
